items.explain: Spearman tolerates some nonlinear monotonic patterns better than Pearson.

choices: Linear association between two continuous variables
items.explain: Pearson measures linear association, not causation.

items.prompt: Example 6.4 warns that nonlinear patterns may need non-linear models even if a linear fit looks tempting.
items.explain: EDA should reveal curvature or clusters before model choice.

items.prompt: Why confirm heatmap pairs with scatter plots?
items.rubric: Verify shape, outliers, nonlinear patterns; correlation ≠ causation.
items.sample_answer: Scatter plots reveal curvature and outliers that a single coefficient can hide.
